title: Downtown Chesterfield Redevelopment
content:
The Staenberg Group is transforming the vacant Chesterfield Mall into a $2 billion mixed-use urban downtown spanning 117 acres.

BACKGROUND: Chesterfield Mall closed August 2024 after reaching 84% vacancy. Sales tax revenue dropped ~70% since 2011, assessed value fell ~90%. Demolition completed August 2025, Dillard's and Macy's buildings preserved.

TIMELINE:
- Infrastructure: 12-18 months
- Vertical construction: early 2027
- First residential move-in: late 2028 or early 2029

HOUSING: Up to 2,363 residential units approved. Could accommodate 5,000-6,000 residents within one mile. Options: apartments, condos, senior living, townhomes.

GREEN SPACE: 3.13-acre central park as centerpiece. 25% of total space dedicated to public plazas, sidewalks, trails, bike lanes, parks. Rain gardens and sustainable water management.

ECONOMIC IMPACT: $353 million in TIF funding:
- $105M public improvements in Downtown Chesterfield
- $25M Wildhorse Village infrastructure
- $168M citywide road/intersection improvements
- $54M for Parkway and Rockwood School Districts

Note: Chesterfield has no city property tax. First TIF approval in 30 years.

VISION: Create vibrant urban center complementing (not competing with) St. Louis City and Clayton.
